title: ranunculus auricomus proles pseudopsis
intro: Ranunculus auricomus proles pseudopsis, also known as the Lesser Celandine, is a perennial plant with yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves that grows in damp, woodland areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of the alkaloids ranunculin and protoanemonin. The health benefits of Lesser Celandine include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Lesser Celandine can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
